name: ChatGPT
category: General AI, agents, coding, multimodal intelligence
bestUseCase: Robotics research workflows, agent planning, coding, content analysis, and multimodal reasoning.
pricing: Free and paid ChatGPT plans, plus API pricing depending on model and usage.
limitations: robotics deployment still depends on external tools, sensors, and control systems
robologaiVerdict: One of the strongest general intelligence layers for robotics teams that need reasoning, coding, and workflow automation in one system.

name: Claude
bestUseCase: Long-form robotics analysis, safety reasoning, product research, and technical documentation.
pricing: Free and paid Claude plans, plus API usage pricing depending on model and product access.
limitations: robotics control and embodied deployment require external infrastructure
robologaiVerdict: A high-signal model for robotics intelligence work where long documents, safety, and careful reasoning matter.

name: Gemini
category: Multimodal AI, research, agents, ecosystem integration
bestUseCase: Multimodal robotics research, video/image analysis, Google ecosystem workflows, and physical AI research context.
pricing: Free and paid Google AI, Workspace, and API access depending on product and region.
limitations: practical robotics deployment depends on product access and external systems
robologaiVerdict: A key model family to watch for multimodal robotics because perception-heavy intelligence is central to Physical AI.

name: Grok
category: Real-time social intelligence, research, conversational AI
bestUseCase: Tracking fast-moving robotics discourse, social signals, market conversation, and public robotics narratives.
pricing: Available through X and xAI access tiers depending on product, region, and account type.
limitations: robotics engineering workflows may require more specialized tooling
robologaiVerdict: Useful for robotics market awareness and public signal tracking, especially where X conversation is part of the story.

name: Perplexity
bestUseCase: Source-backed robotics research, company discovery, news checks, and fast competitive landscape scans.
pricing: Free and paid plans with model access depending on tier.
limitations: less suited for deep robotics coding or embodied agent control by itself
robologaiVerdict: A strong research companion for robotics intelligence when source discovery and fast verification matter most.

name: Mistral
category: Open models, enterprise AI, coding, European AI infrastructure
bestUseCase: Enterprise AI deployments, open-model experimentation, private robotics workflows, and coding support.
pricing: Open-weight models plus hosted platform and API pricing depending on deployment route.
limitations: robotics-specific embodied capabilities depend on downstream integration
robologaiVerdict: Important for robotics teams that care about deployability, sovereignty, and flexible model infrastructure.

name: DeepSeek
bestUseCase: Cost-sensitive coding, reasoning experiments, model comparison, and open AI infrastructure evaluation.
pricing: Open models and API access depending on deployment route and provider.
limitations: multimodal and robotics integration depend heavily on implementation stack
robologaiVerdict: A practical model family for robotics builders testing coding, reasoning, and cost-sensitive AI workflows.

name: Llama
category: Open-weight AI, developer infrastructure, edge and private deployment
bestUseCase: Private robotics systems, edge experiments, open research, and customizable AI infrastructure.
pricing: Open-weight model family; hosting, infrastructure, and provider costs vary by deployment.
limitations: quality and safety depend on implementation, hosting, and fine-tuning choices
robologaiVerdict: One of the most important open AI foundations for robotics teams that want control over deployment and infrastructure.

name: Copilot
category: Productivity AI, coding, enterprise workflows, developer agents
bestUseCase: Developer productivity, enterprise robotics documentation, code assistance, and Microsoft ecosystem workflows.
pricing: Microsoft consumer, business, GitHub, and enterprise plans depending on product.
limitations: robotics intelligence depends on which Copilot product and integrations are used
robologaiVerdict: A strong productivity layer for robotics companies already building inside GitHub and Microsoft workflows.
